title: FAQs
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU’s global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU's campus in New York City.
text:
FAQs
test
First Round Interview Notifications: March 16 - April 15
Second Round Interview Notifications: March 16 - April 15
Admission results will be announced within 30 days after the interview.
Program Dates: July 13, 2026 - July 24, 2026Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:30 AM - 5:30 PM
The program has two application rounds and will close once full. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible.
Our student accommodation is not located directly on campus, so this is not a residential program.However, there is a hotel conveniently located near campus. We are able to provide participants with a special booking discount for this hotel. Reservations are generally made directly between you and the hotel.
The program is delivered in person and includes AI core training, lab project, company visit, tech talk, English and communication session, final presentation, student experience panel, and social activity.
This program is open to high school students aged 15 to 18, regardless of their academic track (arts or sciences). Students with an interest in AI are encouraged to apply.
The program is open to both domestic and international students. Please note, however, that visa support is not provided. International applicants are responsible for ensuring they meet all entry requirements for China.
Yes, students are eligible to apply as long as they are currently enrolled in high school at the time of application.
The selection committee will evaluate applicants based on their submitted materials and interview performance.
While a basic knowledge of Python is a plus, admissions are based on a holistic evaluation. Applicants without prior programming experience are still encouraged to apply. We welcome students with a strong passion for learning and a willingness to grow.
No, this program is not affiliated with NYU Shanghai's undergraduate admissions process. Participation in this program will not influence your chances of admission to NYU Shanghai's undergraduate programs. The undergraduate admissions process operates independently, and applicants must meet NYU Shanghai's official admissions requirements.
The program fee covers tuition, lunch, and materials. It does not include transportation, accommodation, or any other expenses not mentioned.
Refund Policy
Refund Application Timeline and StandardsIf requesting a refund due to personal schedule changes, applications submitted before 12:00 PM (Beijing Time) on June 30, 2026, will receive an 80% refund of the paid fees. Applications submitted after this deadline will not be eligible for a refund.In cases of unexpected illness, family emergencies, or other special circumstances, full refunds will be issued upon submission of appropriate proof.If the program is canceled due to natural disasters or other force majeure events, a full refund will be provided.
If requesting a refund due to personal schedule changes, applications submitted before 12:00 PM (Beijing Time) on June 30, 2026, will receive an 80% refund of the paid fees. Applications submitted after this deadline will not be eligible for a refund.
In cases of unexpected illness, family emergencies, or other special circumstances, full refunds will be issued upon submission of appropriate proof.
If the program is canceled due to natural disasters or other force majeure events, a full refund will be provided.
Refund ProcessSubmit an application via the official email. If the refund request is due to special circumstances, relevant supporting documents (e.g., medical certificates) should be attached.
Submit an application via the official email. If the refund request is due to special circumstances, relevant supporting documents (e.g., medical certificates) should be attached.
